institutions.housing_support_link: Maynooth Studentpad is the off-campus accommodation service that is offered by our Student Services. Our MU Student Services deliver webinars to advise students on how best to search for off-campus accommodation. Students will receive an email invitation to attend these webinars approximately three months prior to their arrival in Ireland. Webinars will cover how to use the portal, as well as tips on how best to search for accommodation, the types of accommodation available and public transport options. Students need only attend one webinar, as all webinars will cover the same content. Students have the option to attend the one that best suits their schedule. Students can attend an online Q&A session if they have any questions prior to their accommodation search. Students will also receive guest login details for the Studentpad portal after the webinars have taken place. https://www.maynoothuniversity.ie/student-services/maynoothstudentpad

institutions.address: 67 Tsukamoto-cho, Fukakusa, Fushimi-ku, Kyoto-shi, Kyoto-fu 612-8577
institutions.fact_sheet_link: We are currently in the process of creating our latest fact sheet and will share it with you as soon as we can.
institutions.incentive_details: Exchange students are given priority for our International Student Dormitories, which are located just a couple of minutes on foot from Fukakusa Campus, where the majority of classes for Exchange Students are held.
institutions.fields_of_study: ["Arts, Media & Creative Studies","Business & Economics "," Cultural Studies","Languages & Linguistics","Social Sciences ","Science and Technology classes are available, but I did not select that option because the majority of subjects require a high level of Japanese language skills. However, if the students have the skills and are willing to travel to Seta Campus, such subjects could be considered. "]
institutions.english_language_requirement: To explain further about the question above, exchange students may opt to focus on Japanese language and subjects taught in Japanese, but we do have a full English-track course available for students who have never studied Japanese before. We do not have a specific "requirement" for the English track, but it would be helpful if students have the equivalency of around TOEFL 500.
institutions.non_english_proficiency: We do not have a specific proficiency requirement, but for students who would like to focus intensely on Japanese, we recommend the equivalency of approximately JLPT N4. Before students' arrival, we will ask the students to take an online placement test, and they will also have an online interview with our Japanese instructors. The instructors will then decide if the student can continue in the Japanese track or should take subjects in the English track for at least their first semester.
institutions.application_documents: ["Academic Transcript ","Immunization / Health Records / Medical Exams","Letter of Recommendation","Passport Copy","Statement of Purpose/Letter of intent","Please refer to this page: https://intl.ryukoku.ac.jp/english/html/s_exchange_02.html"]
institutions.support_services: ["Academic Advising","Counselling","Disability Services","LGBTQ+ Support Services","Medical / Health Clinic","Orientation Programs","Recreation / Gym","Japanese language partners, live-in Resident Supporters (Japanese Students) at the dormitory, and student organizations (Global START, Overseas Exchange Committee) who organize regular friendship events for international students"]
institutions.documentation_required: We need to receive diagnostic documents from the student's doctor. It would also be particularly helpful if we could receive documentation from the home university regarding the accommodations provided to students in the UFV environment. With those documents, we then arrange a meeting with a coordinator for our Office for Students with Disabilities to commence the process of requesting special accommodations from the student's instructors.
institutions.housing_options: Not guaranteed, but students are welcome to apply.
institutions.cost_of_living: We recommend that students have available 80,000 JPY/month.
institutions.insurance_cost_cad: Only the Ward Office has this information, but I believe the cost is around 2,800JPY (about 25 CAD) per month.
institutions.promotional_materials: Please refer to our YouTube account.

institutions.fall_semester_dates: Autumn/winter semester: • Orientation days: Last two weekdays prior to semester start • Classes: 1 September – 31 December • Ordinary exams: 1 January – 31 January (some exams take place in December – please see the course descriptions for more information) • Re-exams: 1 February – 28 February
institutions.winter_semester_dates: Spring semester: • Orientation days: Last two weekdays prior to semester start • Classes: 1 February – 31 May • Ordinary exams: 1 June – 30 June (some exams take place in May – please see the course descriptions for more information) • Re-exams: 1 August – 31 August
institutions.application_documents: ["Application is online – the following documents must be uploaded: • Language Requirement Form • Transcript of records (in English + signed and stamped or digitally verifiable) • Proof of nomination from home university • Copy of passport or national ID card (national ID card: only EU/EEA and Nordic citizens) More information: please see the General Entry Requirements","Proof of Enrollment at Home Institution"]

institutions.english_language_requirement: All students must meet the University’s English language requirements. If you are from an English‑speaking country, you may provide your English results from your High School qualification (or equivalent) to meet the language requirement. For all other students, the English language requirements for undergraduate (Bachelor’s) are as follows: IELTS: overall band score of 6.0, with 6.0 in Writing and Speaking, 5.5 in Reading and Listening OR TOEFL iBT 67 overall with 12 Listening, 12 Reading, 19 Speaking, 19 Writing. For semester‑long exchanges, we can also accept a confirmation letter from the home institution verifying that the nominated student(s) meet UEL’s English language requirements and outlining the assessment methods and scores. The Global Experiences team can assist with drafting this letter if needed.

institutions.fields_of_study: ["Arts, Media & Creative Studies","Business & Economics ","Computing, Engineering and Technology"," Cultural Studies","Health & Human Services","Natural & Physical Sciences","Languages & Linguistics","Social Sciences "]
institutions.english_language_requirement: 1. IELTS - overall band score of at least 6.0 2. TOEFL: ITP or PBT - 550 or higher / computer-based (CBT) - 213 or higher / internet-based (iBT) - 80 or higher 3. TOEIC - 750 or higher 4. Letter from a university professor familiar both with the expected standards of an English-medium university program and with the student's ability to perform in an English-medium academic atmosphere.
institutions.non_english_proficiency: 1. TOPIK 3+ 2. KLAT 150+ (B2) 3. 15 hours (4-5 classes) of university-level Korean language courses (e.g., Korean 101, 102, 201, 202, 301) 4. Letter from a university professor familiar both with the expected standards of a Korean-medium university program and with the student's ability to perform in a Korean-medium academic atmosphere.
